info.description: Official API documentation for Surf - Your gateway to Web3 exploration and discovery.
info.title: Surf API Documentation

post.description: Create a new API key for an existing tenant and create the association
post.summary: Create and associate an API key for a tenant

get.description: List all tenants with type, credit and usage

parameters.description: Page number (>=1)
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: Limit (1-200)
parameters.name: limit

post.description: Create a tenant (optional: also create and return an API key)

get.description: Filter tenant_usage_histories by date, arg, url (all optional); group by arg, url, tenant_id to sum amount, output sorted by amount descending
get.summary: Aggregate tenant_usage_histories by arg, url, tenant_id

parameters.description: Optional, format 2006-01-02 or RFC3339; omit for no filter; when set, filter created_at >= date
parameters.name: date

parameters.description: Optional; omit for no filter, when set filter by arg
parameters.name: arg

parameters.description: Optional; omit for no filter, when set filter by url
parameters.name: url

get.description: Query tenant info by id (UUID) and return the matched tenant (credit & usage included)

parameters.description: tenant_id (UUID)
parameters.name: id

patch.description: Modify tenant by tenant_id. Only updates fields that are provided in the request; omitted fields are left unchanged.

parameters.description: tenant_id (UUID)
parameters.name: id

get.description: Query tenant_usage_histories by tenant_id, group by url and arg to sum amount; optional date param filters created_at >= date
get.summary: Summarize tenant usage by url and arg

parameters.description: tenant_id (UUID)
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Optional, format 2006-01-02 or RFC3339; omit to query all
parameters.name: date

post.description: Authenticate using Google OAuth ID token. Verifies the Google credentials and returns access token and refresh token upon successful authentication. Only works if the email exists in the tenant table.
Authenticate using email and OTP code. Returns access token and refresh token upon successful verification. Only works if the email exists in the tenant table.
Send a one-time password (OTP) code to the specified email address. Only works if the email exists in the tenant table.
Validate refresh token, extend its expiry, and return a new access token along with the same refresh token.

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or expired refresh token

post.description: OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions. Use Bearer authorization. Proxied to hermod for authentication, billing, and LLM execution.

get.description: Query tenant_usage_histories for the authenticated user's tenant in the period that contains the given date (or current period if date omitted). Period is from tenant creation, refreshing every month. Returns tenant credit_limit/10 and type. Only supports Token authentication.
get.summary: Get current user's usage summary by url and arg

parameters.description: Optional, format 2006-01-02 or RFC3339; omit for current period
parameters.name: date

post.description: MAX subscription users can claim an enterprise API key. Each user can only claim once. The plain API key is returned only at creation time.

403.description: User is not a MAX subscriber

get.description: Returns the enterprise API key metadata (prefix, credit limit, status) for the authenticated user. The plain key is NOT returned.

delete.description: Revokes the enterprise API key for the authenticated user. After revocation the user can claim a new key.

get.description: Returns the latest sentiment value for a single symbol from ta_indicator_scores (sourced from sentiment_raw), along with historical changes at 4h, 24h, 7d, and 30d look-back periods using nearest-prior rows.

parameters.description: Canonical symbol (e.g. BTC, ETH)
parameters.name: symbol

parameters.description: Exchange name (default BITHUMB)
parameters.name: exchange

get.description:
Returns the latest BITHUMB batch from ta_indicator_scores —
generated_at + per-symbol scores. Used by bithumb-job's
asset_summary sensor: generated_at drives the sensor's
cursor, assets feed the asset_summary pipeline as its
score_ranking_snapshot. Deliberately bypasses the
asset_summary overlays now served by
/v1/market/ai-recommended-assets so the sensor doesn't
trigger off its own downstream output.

Returns the latest technical indicator values for a single symbol, along with historical changes at 4h, 24h, 7d, and 30d look-back periods. For each period, the API uses the nearest historical row whose open_time is at or before the target look-back timestamp.

parameters.description: Canonical symbol (e.g. BTC, ETH)
parameters.name: symbol

parameters.description: Exchange name (default BITHUMB)
parameters.name: exchange

get.description:
Returns tokens with overall score >= 6.0 drawn from the top-200 by market cap, ranked highest-first. Capped at 50 items.
Overall score = Round(Average(6 individual metric scores), 1).
Each item includes: rank, asset info, overall score, 6 category scores, and a Korean summary (max 20 chars).

parameters.description: Date in YYYY-MM-DD format. If omitted, returns the most recent data
parameters.name: date

get.description: Returns top-200 tokens by market cap with AI scoring and ranking, keyed by all four templates (score_ranking / summary / comprehensive_evaluation / sentiment_trend) in one response.
Returns structured AI analysis from the bithumb_reports table for all signal types in one array.

200.description: Array of objects, one per signal type.

get.description:
AI Report API generates a structured research report for crypto assets, combining real-time market snapshots, trend summaries, technical analysis, sector-optimized on-chain metrics, and community sentiment insights.
The report is generated as a point-in-time snapshot and is designed to be beginner-friendly and neutral, presenting both bullish and bearish factors without providing financial advice.
On-chain indicators are dynamically selected based on the asset’s sector classification using a three-layer metric framework (common, shared, and sector-specific).
If certain data is unavailable or not applicable, corresponding fields will return null or be omitted.

parameters.description: Date in YYYY-MM-DD format. If date is not provided, returns the most recent data
parameters.name: date

parameters.description: Ticker (optional). If omitted, returns reports for all top-200 tokens by market cap
parameters.name: ticker

parameters.description: If true, serves the pre-polish base_report (raw model output) instead of post-polish ai_json_*. Used by bithumb-qa to score raw model output.
parameters.name: raw

200.description: Returns AI reports on success (all top-200)

get.description:
Returns tokens with overall score < 4.0 drawn from the top-200 by market cap, ranked lowest-first. Capped at 50 items.
Overall score = Round(Average(6 individual metric scores), 1).
Each item includes: rank, asset info, overall score, 6 category scores, and a Korean summary (max 20 chars).

parameters.description: Date in YYYY-MM-DD format. If omitted, returns the most recent data
parameters.name: date

get.description: Aggregates the six legacy market sub-endpoints (must-know-news, rise-tokens, drop-tokens, sentiment, trends, events) in one response. Each section is independent — sub-sources do not share a batch_sequence — and missing sub-sources surface as null/empty rather than 404'ing the whole response.

200.description: Returns aggregated market report on success

get.description:
Returns precomputed Token Terminal / CoinGecko metric
series for the Bithumb top-200 cohort. List shape; no
per-symbol query path. Refreshed hourly by bithumb-job;
Korean narrative refreshed daily 07:50 KST.

finish_reason.description:
Indicates why the model stopped generating. Common values:
- stop — normal completion
- length — hit max token limit
- tool_calls — model invoked a tool
- content_filter — blocked by safety filters
- error — unexpected interruption

content.example: Here is a summary of today's Bitcoin market...

reasoning.description: A field to showcase model's thinking process.

created.description: Timestamp indicating when the message was generated.

id.description: A unique identifier for the generated message. Used for internal tracking.

email.description: 用于 Google/OTP 登录，唯一索引，可为空

prices.description: 可选，按 arg 覆盖默认单价，写入 DB 时为 price*10

content.description: The final user-visible text generated by the assistant.

role.description:
The role of the message. The source of the message. Include:
- system — system instructions
- user — user input
- assistant — model output
- tool — tool responses

email.description: 传 null 或空串可清空

prices.description: 按 arg 覆盖单价，写入 DB 时为 price*10

sentiment_trend.description:
SentimentTrend is omitted when ta_indicator_scores.sentiment_raw
is NULL for this row. Asset_summary's prompt builder treats an
absent block as "no sentiment data, skip the sentiment section".

is_sentiment_insufficient_data.description:
IsSentimentInsufficientData reflects Argus's is_insufficient_data flag at the
time the writer captured Sentiment (e.g. <5 mentions over 7d). When true,
Sentiment is NULL by construction. nil means the writer did not record the
flag (legacy rows written before the column existed).

credit.description: Credit 当前可用额度（余额）

credit_limit.description: CreditLimit 额度上限（展示值 = 存库值/10）

usage.description: Usage 累计使用额度（累计消费）

amount_sum.description: amount 总和（与 credit 同单位时已除以 10）
amount 总和（与 credit 同单位时已除以 10）

analysis.description:
Empty string when narrative hasn't run yet or the series is too
short. NEVER null — frontend already handles "".

description.example: Calculate the total value of a crypto portfolio

parameters.description: Parameters is a JSON Schema object.
